input.headline: Dick Cheney funeral: George W. Bush delivers eulogy

raw.headline: Dick Cheney funeral: George W. Bush delivers eulogy

winner: At Dick Cheney's funeral, George W. Bush delivered a 30-minute eulogy — and then asked if anyone had seen his missing middle finger.

candidates.text: I tried to attend Dick Cheney's funeral, but I got turned away because they said "No Bushes allowed.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'Bushes' (the plant) and the Bush family, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: George W. Bush said, "Dick, you were always a bit of a chameleon—today, you're in a casket.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'chameleon' and 'casket' are clever and specific, fitting the context with surprise and brevity."}

candidates.text: At Dick Cheney's funeral, George W. Bush delivered a 30-minute eulogy — and then asked if anyone had seen his missing middle finger.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific reference to Cheney's infamous missing middle finger, concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: George W. Bush gave a eulogy so moving that Dick Cheney’s ghost finally asked for a refund.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with ghost and refund, specific to the characters involved, concise and funny."}

candidates.text: George W. Bush stood at the podium, took a deep breath, and shouted, "I'm sorry, but I'm not authorized to speak for the guy who's currently holding the microphone!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the context of the headline. It uses a clever twist on the situation with natural English and good funniness."}

candidates.text: George W. Bush's eulogy for Dick Cheney was a real masterclass in "over-arching" the truth.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'over-arching' as a play on both 'over-arching' (as in covering up) and Cheney's actual role, with historical specificity and concise delivery."}

candidates.text: At Dick Cheney's funeral, George W. Bush delivered a eulogy so boring, the pallbearers asked if they could take a nap during the rest of the service.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a surprising twist (pallbearers asking to nap). It connects well with the headline and relies on the contrast between Bush's role and the expected solemnity of a funeral."}

candidates.text: George W. Bush stood at the podium and delivered a moving eulogy, though most of the mourners were confused as to why he was still trying to authorize the funeral's existence.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and includes a surprising twist by referencing the historical context of Cheney's role in authorizing military actions."}

candidates.text: During the eulogy, George W. Bush stood at the podium and said, 'Dick, I always knew you were a great shot.' The crowd chuckled nervously as he pulled out his concealed Taser.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke has some surprise and specificity with the Taser reference, but it's somewhat generic and relies on a tired political punchline. The humor is mild and not particularly original."}
